title: agropyron pinifolium var. sclerophyllum
intro: Agropyron pinifolium var. sclerophyllum, also known as needle grass, is a type of perennial grass native to North America, characterized by its needle-like leaves and a robust root system. Traditionally, needle grass has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and minor wounds due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of needle grass include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and support the immune system. Needle grass can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
